[{"url": "https://www.mesabitribune.com/news/business/u-s-steel-airs-potential-impact-of-merger-failure/article_751fec4a-6ae7-11ef-88a3-fbd763eda377.html", "title": "U.S. Steel airs potential impact of merger failure | Business | mesabitribune.com", "content": "United States Steel Corporation says it would pivot away from blast furnace operations and potentially move its headquarters if its proposed merger with Nippon Steel Corporation fails.

After months of criticism from the United Steelworkers union over the proposed $14.9 billion dollar merger, it’s the first time that the iconic steelmaker has publicly talked about possible impacts should the merger not be approved by federal regulators.", "score": 0.93309647, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.businessnorth.com/daily_briefing/u-s-steel-voices-ramifications-of-no-deal-with-nippon-steel/article_b8155050-6ac7-11ef-b280-b7885f078368.html", "title": "U.S. Steel voices ramifications of no deal with Nippon Steel | The Daily Briefing | businessnorth.com", "content": "# U.S. Steel voices ramifications of no deal with Nippon Steel. says it would move away from its blast furnace operations and potentially move its headquarters if its proposed merger with Nippon Steel Corporation fails. An employee rally in support of the deal is planned Wednesday at the United States Steel Tower in Pittsburgh, according to the company. "Today's rally is about displaying support for the transaction with Nippon Steel," David Burritt, U.S. Steel president and chief executive", "score": 0.89121616, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2024-09-04/us-steel-warns-nippon-deal-failure-would-put-thousands-jobs-risk", "title": "US Steel warns Nippon deal failure would put thousands of jobs at risk | Kitco News", "content": "He said that without the deal, “US Steel will largely pivot away from its blast furnace facilities, putting thousands of good-paying union jobs at risk (and) negatively impacting numerous communities across the locations where its facilities exist.”

He added that the failure of the deal would raise “serious questions about US Steel remaining headquartered in Pittsburgh.” He told the Wall Street Journal it would likely move its headquarters to the south if the deal collapsed.", "score": 0.8674071, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.livemint.com/companies/failure-of-us-steel-nippon-deal-may-put-thousands-of-jobs-at-risk-report-11725469964601.html", "title": "Failure of US Steel-Nippon deal may put thousands of jobs at risk | Company Business News", "content": ""US Steel will largely pivot away from its blast furnace facilities, putting thousands of good-paying union jobs at risk (and) negatively impacting numerous communities across the locations where its facilities exist,” CEO Burritt said.

The failure of the deal would raise "serious questions about US Steel remaining headquartered in Pittsburgh,” he added.", "score": 0.8482825, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://nypost.com/2024/09/04/business/us-steel-will-close-plants-move-hq-from-pittsburgh-if-nippon-sale-fails-ceo-warns", "title": "US Steel will cut jobs, close plants, move HQ from Pittsburgh if Nippon sale fails, CEO warns", "content": "He said that without the deal, “US Steel will largely pivot away from its blast furnace facilities, putting thousands of good-paying union jobs at risk (and) negatively impacting numerous communities across the locations where its facilities exist.”

US Steel shares rose more than 1% on Wednesday.

Edgar Thomson Plant in Braddock, Pennsylvania with large smokestacks and trucks parked outside, symbol of 2024 political controversy over its buying by Nippon Steel Corp.  6", "score": 0.83742094, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.visiontimes.com/2024/09/04/us-steel-ceo-warns-of-job-cuts-plant-closures-should-nippon-acquisition-fail.html", "title": "US Steel CEO Warns of Job Cuts, Plant Closures Should Nippon Acquisition Fail - Vision Times", "content": "On Sept. 4, US Steel warned that should its merger with Nippon Steel fail, it would jeopardize thousands of union jobs, likely result in plant closures, and US Steel would relocate its headquarters out of Pennsylvania.

The stark warning came amidst growing bipartisan opposition to the deal and following comments by Democratic Vice President Kamala Harris on Monday, Sept. 2, when she said that she wanted US Steel to remain “American owned and operated.”", "score": 0.78532547, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.wfyi.org/wfyi-news/2024-12-13/nippon-to-revamp-polluting-blast-furnace-in-gary-as-part-of-us-steel-merger", "title": "Nippon to revamp polluting blast furnace in Gary as part of U.S. Steel merger", "content": "The Gary Works plant in northwest Indiana is U.S. Steel's largest steel manufacturing facility.

Nippon Steel Corporation has no plans to make steelmaking greener in Gary as part of its merger with U.S. Steel.

In a press conference with the city of Gary on Thursday, Nippon’s Vice Chairman Takahiro Mori said Nippon plans to spend $300 million to revamp one of the blast furnaces at Gary Works.", "score": 0.46366343, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.spglobal.com/energy/en/news-research/latest-news/metals/111121-algoma-to-construct-eafs-to-replace-blast-furnace-steelmaking-by-2024", "title": "Algoma to construct EAFs to replace blast furnace steelmaking by 2024 | S&P Global", "content": "related to its recent merger with Legato Merger. Once the EAFs come online in 2024, Algoma said it expects to gradually transition away from its blast furnace steelmaking as more electric power on the grid supplying its operations becomes available.", "score": 0.2456822, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://gmk.center/en/news/us-steel-kosice-resumed-operation-of-the-blast-furnace-after-a-month-and-a-half-of-downtime", "title": "US Steel Kosice has resumed operation of blast furnace number two - News of European metallurgy", "content": "icon

 All news", "score": 0.14688116, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.steelmarketupdate.com/2014/02/25/ak-steel-ashland-blast-furnace-down", "title": "AK Steel Ashland Blast Furnace Down - Steel Market Update", "content": "the Company will utilize its Middletown Works blast furnace and its Butler Works electric arc furnace, as well as purchase merchant carbon slabs, to service its customers.”", "score": 0.10274522, "raw_content": null}]
